nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cockburn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Midlothian) and northern English: habitational name from a place in Berwickshire named Cockburn, from Old English cocc ‘cock, rooster’ (or the related byname Cocca) + burna ‘stream’ (see Bourne ). This surname is traditionally pronounced Coburn.

variants.name: Bourne
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from southern Middle English bourne, Old English burna, burne ‘spring, stream’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example Bourn in Cambridgeshire or Bourne in Lincolnshire. In surnames the reference is often to an old stream called burna, surviving as the name of a farm. This word was replaced as the general word for a stream in southern dialects by Old English brōc (see Brook ) and came to be restricted in meaning to a stream flowing only intermittently, especially in winter.

variants.name: Coburn
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Cockburn  , reflecting the pronunciation.
Altered form of German Kobern.

variants.name: Cobourn
meanings: Scottish and English (Staffordshire): variant of   Cockburn  .

variants.name: Cogburn
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Cockburn  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: that
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Sanjay, Gajanan, Narayan, Narendra, Dinesh, Ganesh, Hari, Kiran, Narayana, Seema, Shrikant, Suresh.
Indian: variant of   Bhatt  , common in the southern states of India.

variants.name: Bhatt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Bharat, Kiran, Harish, Nikhil, Praful, Ashok, Atul, Dilip, Harshad, Jitendra, Mahesh, Rajesh.
Indian: Brahmin name, from Sanskrit bhaṭṭa ‘lord’, based on bhartr- ‘lord, husband’, a derivative of bhar- ‘to bear, support’. See also Bhatti and Bhatia .

variants.name: Bhatti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohammad, Muhammad, Mohammed, Abdul, Javed, Rashid, Zia, Hamid, Imtiaz, Khalid, Shahid, Ahsan.  Indian Awtar, Nanak, Niranjan, Ajit, Amrit, Anuradha, Chander, Deepak, Dharam, Dinesh, Harjeet, Jagat.

Pakistani and Indian (Punjab, Rajasthan): Muslim, Rajput, and Sikh name, probably derived from the eponymous ancestor of the Bhatti tribe. Etymologically, the name is related to Sanskrit bhaṭṭa ‘lord’ (see Bhat ). This tribe is by far the largest and most widely distributed of the Rajput tribes of the Punjab. There is also an area in the Punjab called Bhattiana, which was once ruled by the Bhattis. Bhatti occurs as a personal name in Sanskrit literature. It was the name of a well-known Sanskrit poet of the 6th century. See also Bhatt and Bhatia .

nameMeanings.name: Ilk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Norfolk): nickname from Middle English milk ‘milk’ (Old English meolc). There are many possible reasons for the use of such a nickname, including milk-white hair, the collecting or selling of milk, and of course the drinking of milk – whether or not taken to indicate effeminacy or spiritlessness.

Sorbian: from a pet form of a personal name such as Miłosław, based on the Slavic element mil ‘gentle, dear’ (from Old Slavic milъ). This surname is found mainly in Germanized forms, such as Mielke and Milke .
Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Milch   ‘milk’.

variants.name: Mielke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erwin, Erna, Kurt, Reinhard, Arno, Erhardt, Ernst, Ewald, Frieda, Fritz.
German (of Slavic origin): from a Germanized pet form of any of various Old Slavic personal names, such as Milogost, based on the element mil ‘gentle, dear’ (from Old Slavic milъ); compare 3 below.
North German: from a pet form of the personal name   Miele   4, of ancient Germanic origin.

variants.name: Milks
meanings: English: variant of Milk , with excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Canadian Hénault or directly of French Hunault (see Henault 2).
Catalan (Añó): Castilianized form of Anyó, probably from the old personal name Annone.
Japanese: variant of   Anno  .

variants.name: Anno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Masayuki, Tadashi, Yoshito.
Japanese: written 安野 ‘peaceful plain’. This surname is rare in Japan proper, and may have originated in the Ryūkyū Islands, where it is pronounced   Ano  .

variants.name: Henault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Lucien, Martial, Pierre, Camille, Fernand, Laurier, Monique, Normand, Philippe.
French (Hénault): from the ancient Germanic personal name Haginwald, composed of the elements hagin ‘enclosure, protected place’ and wald ‘power, authority’.
Altered form (Hénault) of French Hunault: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hunwald, composed of the elements hun ‘bear cub’ and wald ‘power, authority’. Compare Ano and Eno .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Robert de Cockburn of that Ilk and Henderland was born in 1210, in Scotland, United Kingdom as the son of Piers de Cockburn. He had at least 3 sons with Cockburn.

fr.lifeSummary: Robert de Cockburn of that Ilk and Henderland est né en 1210, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, fils de Piers de Cockburn. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Cockburn.

de.lifeSummary: Robert de Cockburn of that Ilk and Henderland wurde 1210 in Schottland, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Piers de Cockburn geboren. Er hatte mit Cockburn mindestens 3 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Robert de Cockburn of that Ilk and Henderland è nato il nel 1210 nel Scozia, Regno Unito come figlio di Piers de Cockburn. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Cockburn.

ru.lifeSummary: Robert de Cockburn of that Ilk and Henderland родился в 1210 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Piers de Cockburn. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Cockburn.
